clinical_coherence.notes: 25F East Asian nurse with PCOS and iron deficiency anemia, presenting with NMDAR encephalitis initially appearing psychiatric. Clinical picture is coherent. The nurse occupation adds a unique element (healthcare worker may self-diagnose or delay seeking care). However, this is the third moderate case with the same PCOS/asthma PMH template, same family autoimmune history, and identical CSF WBC count of 12.

tool_output_consistency.notes: CSF shows mild pleocytosis (12 cells), elevated protein (49.1), borderline glucose (56.2). EEG with extreme delta brush. All concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G04.81 correct. Critical actions clinically appropriate.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Appropriately moderate with pre-existing psychiatric confounding and mild CSF.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 25F East Asian nurse is a realistic and diverse demographic.

educational_value.notes: Third moderate NMDAR case with identical template. CSF WBC of 12 in all three moderate cases is a conspicuous lack of variation. Limited additional teaching value beyond M01.

issues_found: Third moderate NMDAR case with identical CSF WBC (12 cells), same PMH template (PCOS, asthma), and same family history pattern
Exact CSF WBC of 12 across all three moderate cases is unrealistic and suggests batch generation without sufficient randomization
strengths: East Asian demographic and nurse occupation add diversity
Iron deficiency anemia history is a unique PMH element
Tetracycline allergy (photosensitivity) is a realistic and unusual allergy
